title: Quiz: Holographic Quantum Error Correction
description: Test your understanding of holographic codes, AdS/CFT, and the emergence of spacetime from entanglement

questions.question: The Ryu-Takayanagi formula relates what two quantities?

options.text: Energy and temperature
Entanglement entropy of a boundary region and the area of a minimal bulk surface
Entropy and volume of a region
Momentum and position uncertainty

questions.explanation: The Ryu-Takayanagi formula states that S(A) = Area(gamma_A)/(4*G_N), where S(A) is the entanglement entropy of boundary region A and gamma_A is the minimal surface in the bulk that is homologous to A. This reveals a deep connection between quantum information (entanglement) and geometry (area).

questions.question: In holographic quantum error correction, the 'bulk' corresponds to what in a standard QEC code?

options.text: Physical qubits
Syndrome measurements
Logical (encoded) information
Error operators

questions.explanation: In holographic codes, bulk operators correspond to logical operators acting on the encoded information. The boundary degrees of freedom are the physical qubits, and bulk information is protected against erasure of boundary regions - the hallmark structure of quantum error correction.

questions.question: What is the entanglement wedge E(A) of a boundary region A?

options.text: The region on the boundary adjacent to A
The bulk region bounded by A and its RT surface, from which A can reconstruct operators
The complement of A on the boundary
The region with maximum entanglement with A

questions.explanation: The entanglement wedge E(A) is the bulk domain of dependence of any surface bounded by region A and its Ryu-Takayanagi surface. Crucially, any bulk operator within E(A) can be represented purely in terms of boundary operators in A - this is the entanglement wedge reconstruction theorem.

questions.question: What does the ER=EPR conjecture propose?

options.text: Energy equals mass times the speed of light squared
Entangled particles are connected by wormholes (Einstein-Rosen bridges)
Entanglement reduces with distance
Black holes have no hair

questions.explanation: The ER=EPR conjecture (Maldacena and Susskind) proposes that entangled quantum states (EPR pairs) are connected by Einstein-Rosen bridges (wormholes). This suggests that entanglement IS spacetime connectivity - a profound statement about the nature of spacetime at the quantum level.

questions.question: In holographic codes, a bulk operator can only be reconstructed from one specific boundary region.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: False. A key feature of holographic codes is that the SAME bulk operator can often be reconstructed from multiple, even complementary, boundary regions. This redundancy is what provides error protection - the bulk information is not tied to any specific boundary location.

questions.question: The HaPPY code is built from what type of tensors?

options.text: Random tensors
Perfect tensors arranged in hyperbolic geometry
Identity tensors
Projection operators

questions.explanation: The HaPPY (Harlow-Preskill-Pastawski-Yoshida) code is constructed from perfect tensors - tensors that are unitary when contracted on any half of their legs - arranged in a hyperbolic tiling (like {5,4}). This structure naturally produces holographic properties including area-law entanglement and bulk reconstruction.

questions.question: According to the holographic principle, how does the maximum entropy in a region scale?

options.text: With the volume of the region
With the area of the boundary of the region
With the logarithm of the volume
It is constant regardless of size

questions.explanation: The holographic principle states that the maximum entropy in a region scales with the area of its boundary, not its volume: S_max ~ A/(4*l_P^2). This is realized in black hole entropy and is a key principle underlying AdS/CFT. It suggests that physics is fundamentally lower-dimensional than it appears.

questions.question: Which of the following are features of the AdS/CFT correspondence? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Gravity in the bulk corresponds to a non-gravitational theory on the boundary
The boundary theory has one fewer spatial dimension than the bulk
Strong coupling in one theory maps to weak coupling in the other
The two theories describe different physics entirely

questions.explanation: AdS/CFT is a duality where: (a) quantum gravity in AdS corresponds to a non-gravitational CFT on the boundary; (b) the CFT has one fewer spatial dimension (holography); (c) it's a strong-weak duality, making strongly coupled problems tractable. The theories describe the SAME physics, not different physics (d).
